summary: Updated the repository Python dependency constraint for GitPython in requirements.txt from an exact pin of 3.1.30 to a minimum version of 3.1.32 to address a vulnerability. The PR was a small dependency/security maintenance change with no code review discussion in the provided context.
what_changed: Changed GitPython dependency in requirements.txt from GitPython==3.1.30 to GitPython>=3.1.32.
The change relaxed the dependency from an exact pin to a lower-bound constraint while ensuring versions below 3.1.32 are not used.
No project source files, decompilation code, headers, or assembly-matching logic were changed.
smart_moves: Used the minimum safe GitPython version indicated by the vulnerability fix rather than only bumping to another older exact pin.
Kept the PR narrowly scoped to a one-line dependency update, minimizing risk and making the security intent easy to audit.
decomp_lessons: Security and tooling dependency updates should be isolated from decompilation changes so matching-related diffs remain reviewable.
When a Python tool dependency has a vulnerable version, updating requirements.txt alone can be sufficient if no code changes are needed.
For repository maintenance PRs, the key reusable pattern is a focused requirements-only change with a title that states the security motivation.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs that update requirements.txt for security vulnerabilities
Find dependency updates that change exact pins to minimum-version constraints
Audit current GitPython constraint and confirm whether an exact pin or lower-bound policy is preferred
